rubric: See ground_truth/RUBRIC.md — label for DURATION budgeting, not literary theory.

labels.visual_event: Notes: Period live-action Victorian fantasy; humans and talking animals share one stylized live-action medium. ALICE: about seven, fair hair, knee-length blue dress, white apron, striped stockings. WHITE RABBIT: white fur, pink eyes, waistcoat, pocket-watch. Recurring places keep identical heading names.
She considers, solemn.

prev_beat.dialogue: but then I wonder what Latitude or Longitude I've got to?

next_beat.dialogue: I wonder if I shall fall right through the earth!

labels.visual_event: A long, low hall lit by a row of lamps hanging from the roof. Doors all round. Alice tries every one: all locked. She walks sadly down the middle. A little three-legged table of solid glass stands alone. On it: a tiny golden key. She tries the key on the doors - locks too large, or key too small. On a second circuit she finds a low curtain and, behind it, a little door about fifteen inches high. The golden key fits. She opens the door onto a small passage no larger than a rat-hole. Beyond: the loveliest garden - bright flower-beds, cool fountains. She kneels, longing, but cannot get even her head through.

next_beat.dialogue: And even if my head would go through, it would be of very little use without my …

labels.setting: INT. LONG LOW HALL - MOMENTS LATER
labels.visual_event: She shoots upward, opening out like the largest telescope.

prev_beat.dialogue: Curiouser and curiouser!

next_beat.dialogue: Now I'm opening out like the largest telescope that ever was! Good-bye, feet!

labels.setting: INT. LONG LOW HALL - MOMENTS LATER
labels.visual_event: She ticks off children she knows.

prev_beat.dialogue: Ah, that's the great puzzle!

next_beat.dialogue: I'm sure I'm not Ada, for her hair goes in such long ringlets, and mine doesn't …

labels.setting: INT. LONG LOW HALL - MOMENTS LATER
labels.visual_event: She runs to the little door - shut again; the golden key lies on the glass table as before.

prev_beat.dialogue: That was a narrow escape! And now for the garden!

next_beat.dialogue: And things are worse than ever, for I never was so small as this before, never!

labels.visual_event: The Mouse bristles all over.

prev_beat.dialogue: a capital one for catching mice - oh, I beg your pardon!

next_beat.dialogue: We won't talk about her any more if you'd rather not.

labels.setting: EXT. BANK OF THE POOL - DAY
labels.visual_event: They sit in a large ring, Mouse in the middle. Alice watches anxiously, sure of a bad cold if she stays wet.

prev_beat.dialogue: Sit down, all of you, and listen to me! I'll soon make you dry enough!

next_beat.dialogue: Ahem! Are you all ready? This is the driest thing I know. Silence all round, if …

labels.setting: EXT. BANK OF THE POOL - DAY
labels.visual_event: The Dodo receives the thimble solemnly, then presents it back to Alice, saying -

prev_beat.dialogue: Hand it over here.

next_beat.dialogue: We beg your acceptance of this elegant thimble.

labels.setting: EXT. BANK OF THE POOL - DAY
labels.visual_event: Alice guesses at once: the fan and the white kid gloves. She hunts about good-naturedly. Nothing. The great hall, the glass table, the little door - all vanished. The bank and wood have changed since the swim. The Rabbit notices her and calls out in an angry tone.

prev_beat.dialogue: She'll get me executed, as sure as ferrets are ferrets! Where CAN I have dropped…

next_beat.dialogue: Why, Mary Ann, what ARE you doing out here?

labels.setting: INT. WHITE RABBIT'S TIDY LITTLE ROOM - DAY
labels.visual_event: The bottle has had its full effect. She grows no larger - still very uncomfortable, wedged in the little room.

prev_beat.dialogue: Now I can do no more, whatever happens. What WILL become of me?

next_beat.dialogue: It was much pleasanter at home, when one wasn't always growing larger and smalle…

labels.setting: EXT. WHITE RABBIT'S HOUSE - GARDEN - DAY
labels.visual_event: Long silence. Whispers.

prev_beat.dialogue: Well, it's got no business there, at any rate: go and take it away!

next_beat.dialogue: Sure, I don't like it, yer honour, at all, at all!

labels.setting: INT. WHITE RABBIT'S TIDY LITTLE ROOM - DAY
labels.visual_event: She gives one sharp kick.

prev_beat.dialogue: This is Bill.

labels.setting: INT. WHITE RABBIT'S TIDY LITTLE ROOM - DAY
labels.visual_event: Another dead silence outside. Alice notices with surprise: the pebbles on the floor are all turning into little cakes.

prev_beat.dialogue: You'd better not do that again!

next_beat.dialogue: If I eat one of these cakes, it's sure to make SOME change in my size;

labels.visual_event: She looks all round at the flowers and blades of grass. Nothing that looks like the right thing. A large mushroom grows near her, about the same height as herself. She looks under it, on both sides, behind it - then stretches up on tiptoe and peeps over the edge. Her eyes meet those of a large CATERPILLAR, sitting on the top with its arms folded, quietly smoking a long hookah, taking not the smallest notice of her.

prev_beat.dialogue: I suppose I ought to eat or drink something or other; but the great question is,…

labels.visual_event: "In my youth," Father William replied to his son, "I feared it might injure the brain; But, now that I'm perfectly sure I have none, Why, I do it again and again." "You are old," said the youth, "as I mentioned before, And have grown most uncommonly fat; Yet you turned a back-somersault in at the door - Pray, what is the reason of that?" "In my youth," said the sage, as he shook his grey locks, "I kept all my limbs very supple By the use of this ointment - one shilling the box - Allow me to sell you a couple?" "You are old," said the youth, "and your jaws are too weak For anything tougher than suet; Yet you finished the goose, with the bones and the beak - Pray, how did you manage to do it?" "In my youth," said his father, "I took to the law, And argued each case with my wife; And the muscular strength, which it gave to my jaw, Has lasted the rest of my life." "You are old," said the youth, "one would hardly suppose That your eye was as steady as ever; Yet you balanced an eel on the end of your nose - What made you so awfully clever?" "I have answered three questions, and that is enough," Said his father; "don't give yourself airs! Do you think I can listen all day to such stuff? Be off, or I'll kick you down stairs!"

prev_beat.dialogue: And yet you incessantly stand on your head - Do you think, at your age, it is ri…

next_beat.dialogue: That is not said right.

labels.visual_event: Alice's head frees itself. She is delighted - until she finds her shoulders gone; all she can see when she looks down is an immense length of neck, rising like a stalk out of a sea of green leaves far below.

next_beat.dialogue: What can all that green stuff be? And where have my shoulders got to?

labels.visual_event: Alice stands before the door, wondering what to do next, when suddenly a footman in livery comes running out of the wood - the face of a fish, powdered hair drawn into a bag - and raps loudly at the door with his knuckles. It is opened by another FOOTMAN with a round face and large eyes like a frog. Both have powdered hair curled all round their heads. Alice watches with great curiosity. The Fish-Footman produces from under his arm a great letter, nearly as large as himself, and hands it over.

next_beat.dialogue: For the Duchess. An invitation from the Queen to play croquet.

labels.visual_event: She says it aloud, half to herself. The Duchess only scowls. The cook takes no notice.

prev_beat.dialogue: There's certainly too much pepper in that soup!

next_beat.dialogue: Please would you tell me why your cat grins like that?

labels.setting: EXT. WOOD NEAR DUCHESS'S HOUSE - DAY
labels.visual_event: Alice nurses the baby as she walks.

next_beat.dialogue: Don't grunt. That's not at all a proper way of expressing yourself.

labels.setting: EXT. WOOD NEAR DUCHESS'S HOUSE - DAY
labels.visual_event: This time it vanishes quite slowly, beginning with the end of the tail, and ending with the grin, which remains some time after the rest of it has gone.

prev_beat.dialogue: All right.

next_beat.dialogue: Well! I've often seen a cat without a grin, but a grin without a cat!

labels.visual_event: (aloud) I believe I can guess that.

prev_beat.dialogue: Come, we shall have some fun now! I'm glad they've begun asking riddles.

next_beat.dialogue: Do you mean that you think you can find out the answer to it?

labels.visual_event: The Hatter shakes his head mournfully.

prev_beat.dialogue: Is that the way you manage?

next_beat.dialogue: Not I! We quarrelled last March - just before he went mad, you know -

labels.visual_event: He moves on. The Dormouse follows him. The March Hare moves into the Dormouse's place. Alice rather unwillingly takes the place of the March Hare. The March Hare has just upset the milk-jug into his plate; Alice is worse off than before.

prev_beat.dialogue: I want a clean cup. Let's all move one place on.

next_beat.dialogue: But I don't understand. Where did they draw the treacle from?

labels.visual_event: Once more she finds herself in the long hall, close to the little glass table.

next_beat.dialogue: Now, I'll manage better this time.

labels.visual_event: The Knave only bows and smiles.

prev_beat.visual: Queen Of Hearts (severely, to the Knave).
prev_beat.dialogue: Who is this?

next_beat.dialogue: Idiot!

labels.visual_event: The Knave does so, very carefully, with one foot.

prev_beat.dialogue: Turn them over!

next_beat.dialogue: Get up!

labels.visual_event: Alice walks beside the White Rabbit, who peeps anxiously into her face.

next_beat.dialogue: It's - it's a very fine day!

labels.visual_event: The King and executioner argue whether a head can be beheaded without a body. The Cat's head begins to fade away. By the time they have settled anything, the head is entirely gone. The Queen stamps off, shouting "Off with his head!" at anyone who happens to be in her way. Alice thinks she had better go back and see how the game is going on. She hears the Queen's voice in the distance, screaming with passion. She has already heard her sentence three of the players to be executed for having missed their turns. Alice leaves the croquet-ground. She finds the Duchess's arm slipped through hers, and the Duchess smiling in a pleasant way.

prev_beat.dialogue: However, it may kiss my hand, if it likes.

next_beat.dialogue: You can't think how glad I am to see you again, you dear old thing!

labels.visual_event: As they walk off together, Alice hears the King say in a low voice to the company generally -

prev_beat.dialogue: Come on, then, and he shall tell you his history.

next_beat.dialogue: You are all pardoned.

labels.visual_event: He sighs deeply, drawing the back of one flapper across his eyes. He tries to speak; sobs choke his voice.

prev_beat.dialogue: Once, I was a real Turtle.

next_beat.dialogue: Same as if he had a bone in his throat.

labels.visual_event: The Gryphon shakes him and punches him in the back. At last the Mock Turtle recovers.

prev_beat.dialogue: Same as if he had a bone in his throat.

next_beat.dialogue: You may not have lived much under the sea -

labels.visual_event: Alice looks down at her shoes.

prev_beat.dialogue: Why, what are YOUR shoes done with? I mean, what makes them so shiny?

next_beat.dialogue: They're done with blacking, I believe.

labels.visual_event: The Panther took pie-crust, and gravy, and meat, While the Owl had the dish as its share of the treat. When the pie was all finished, the Owl, as a boon, Was kindly permitted to pocket the spoon: While the Panther received knife and fork with a growl, And concluded the banquet -

prev_beat.dialogue: a pie -

next_beat.dialogue: What IS the use of repeating all that stuff, if you don't explain it as you go o…

labels.visual_event: The KING OF HEARTS and QUEEN OF HEARTS sit on their throne. A great crowd of little birds and beasts and the whole pack of cards assembles about them. The KNAVE OF HEARTS stands before them in chains, a SOLDIER on each side. Near the King stands the WHITE RABBIT, trumpet in one hand, scroll of parchment in the other. In the middle of the court: a table with a large dish of tarts. Alice and the Gryphon arrive. Alice eyes the tarts hungrily. Alice looks about, pleased to know the names of things. The King wears his crown over a great wig and does not look comfortable. Twelve jurors - animals and birds mixed - write busily on slates in the jury-box. Among them: BILL THE LIZARD.

next_beat.dialogue: What are they doing? They can't have anything to put down yet, before the trial'…

labels.visual_event: The jury makes a memorandum.

prev_beat.visual: King Of Hearts (turning to the jury).
prev_beat.dialogue: Stolen!

next_beat.dialogue: I keep them to sell: I've none of my own. I'm a hatter.

labels.visual_event: One of the GUINEA-PIGS cheers and is immediately suppressed by officers: they slip him head first into a large canvas bag, tie it up, and sit upon it. Alice watches with interest.

prev_beat.dialogue: You're a very poor speaker.

next_beat.dialogue: If that's all you know about it, you may stand down.

labels.visual_event: A general confusion. The Queen has the Dormouse turned out of court. The whole jury has to write down all the dates on their slates, and then add them up.

prev_beat.dialogue: Treacle.

next_beat.dialogue: Never mind!

labels.visual_event: The King turns pale, and shuts his note-book hastily.

prev_beat.dialogue: Then it ought to be Number One.

next_beat.dialogue: Consider your verdict.

labels.visual_event: He turns to the Knave. The Knave shakes his head sadly.

prev_beat.visual: King Of Hearts (running his eye over the verses).
prev_beat.dialogue: "- said I could not swim -" you can't swim, can you?

next_beat.dialogue: Do I look like it?
